Title: A Cotton Laccase Confers Disease Resistance Against Verticillium dahliae by Promoting Cell Wall Lignification

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Gossypium hirsutum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (Arabidopsis overexpression; Nicotiana transient expression)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and resistance against Verticillium dahliae/Verticillium wilt
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem vascular tissues/cell wall; roots; Nicotiana leaves in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal oxidative polymerization of monolignols in cell wall lignification
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: laccase superfamily domain; multicopper oxidase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ubiquitously expressed, relatively higher in stems; significantly induced at 0.5, 4 and 8 h after V. dahliae inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: oxidized monolignol radicals leading to lignin polymer deposition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes lignin monomers to promote polymerization and deposition of lignin in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to Verticillium dahliae; silencing reduces lignin deposition and disease resistance, whereas overexpression increases lignin content and resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in cotton; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RT-qPCR; histochemical lignin staining; lignin quantification; disease index and fungal biomass assays; subcellular localization; Y2H/BiFC/LCI interaction assays
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhMAPKKK2; AtMAPKKK2 (functional dependence via silencing in OE background)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhLAC14-3 is induced rapidly by V. dahliae infection and is highly expressed in stems. 2. Silencing GhLAC14-3 in cotton increases Verticillium wilt susceptibility, raises fungal biomass, and decreases lignin deposition. 3. GhLAC14-3 overexpression in Arabidopsis increases lignin content and enhances resistance to V. dahliae. 4. GhLAC14-3 interacts with GhMAPKKK2 at the plasma membrane, and this interaction is required for full lignin-promoting activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target for breeding or engineering Verticillium wilt-resistant cotton by enhancing lignification; may be combined with GhMAPKKK2 for stronger effect
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes GhMAPKKK2 may phosphorylate GhLAC14-3 based on interaction and predicted phosphorylation sites, but direct phosphorylation is not experimentally confirmed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Gossypium hirsutum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (Arabidopsis overexpression; Nicotiana transient co-expression)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall strengthening and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem/root-associated lignified tissues; Nicotiana leaves in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: MAP kinase kinase kinase / serine-threonine protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MAPKKK with serine/threonine kinase catalytic domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in roots, stems, and leaves, higher in stems and leaves; significantly induced at 0.5, 4 and 8 h after V. dahliae inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: defense-induced lignin biosynthesis/lignification and disease resistance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein interaction with probable phosphorylation-mediated activation of GhLAC14-3; activation of defense gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhLAC14-3; resistance-related genes including AtNDR1; AtRIN4; AtRPM1; AtMIR399; AtRPW8; AtJAV1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enhances GhLAC14-3-mediated lignin production; overexpression upregulates AtNDR1, AtRIN4, AtRPM1, AtMIR399, AtRPW8 and AtJAV1 in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms an interaction pair/complex with GhLAC14-3 at the plasma membrane
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhLAC14-3; AtMAPKKK2 (homolog functionally tested by silencing)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: required for full GhLAC14-3-mediated lignin accumulation and disease resistance; co-expression with GhLAC14-3 further increases lignin and silencing AtMAPKKK2 suppresses the GhLAC14-3 overexpression phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances resistance to Verticillium dahliae and promotes lignin accumulation in cooperation with GhLAC14-3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H, BiFC, LCI, overexpression, VIGS, lignin quantification, disease assays, RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhMAPKKK2 is membrane-localized and rapidly induced by V. dahliae infection. 2. GhMAPKKK2 physically interacts with GhLAC14-3 at the plasma membrane. 3. Overexpression of GhMAPKKK2 in Arabidopsis enhances resistance to V. dahliae and induces multiple defense genes. 4. Co-expression of GhMAPKKK2 with GhLAC14-3 increases lignin more than GhLAC14-3 alone, and silencing AtMAPKKK2 in GhLAC14-3-OE plants reduces lignin and resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator for engineering Verticillium wilt resistance, potentially in combination with GhLAC14-3 to boost lignification and defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct phosphorylation of GhLAC14-3 by GhMAPKKK2 is hypothesized but not experimentally demonstrated in this study.
